name: Way of the Cid: Castillejo de Robledo Loop
name_original: Castillo de Andaluz y Iglesia de Nuestra Señora – running circular por el Camino del Cid desde Castilllejo de Robledo
description: Explore history and nature on this 5.0-mile (8.0 km) moderate jogging route from Castillejo de Robledo, passing Castillo de Andaluz and Igle
region_intro: Jogging routes around Burgo De Osma traverse a landscape characterized by the confluence of the Ucero and Abión rivers, offering diverse terrain for runners. The region features river valleys, gentle hills, and the dramatic Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park nearby. Paths often follow riverbanks or lead through areas with views of historic sites like Osma Castle. Best jogging routes around Burgo De Osma The most popular jogging route is Gullurías Trail, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 21 minutes to complete. This route offers views towards the Cañón del Río Lobos. Another top favourite among local runners is Río Abión Gorge and La Pedriza Trail, a moderate 6.1 miles (9.9 km) path. This route winds through the scenic Gorge of the River Abión. Local runners also love the Way of the Cid: Castillejo de Robledo Loop, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail leading through varied rural landscapes, often completed in about 55 minutes. Jogging around Burgo De Osma is defined by river valleys, historic town surroundings, and the nearby Cañón del Río Lobos. The network offers options ranging from easy riverside paths to more challenging routes with moderate elevation changes. The routes in Burgo De Osma are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.3 stars from 3 reviews. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Burgo De Osma's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Burgo De Osma?
A: There are over 20 dedicated running routes around Burgo De Osma, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels to suit different preferences.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in the area?
A: Yes, for those looking for a gentle run, there is an &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38161964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;easy running loop from El Burgo de Osma&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features many short walks with little unevenness, particularly along the Ucero and Abión rivers, which are perfect for a refreshing jog.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the running trails near Burgo De Osma?
A: The running routes offer diverse scenery, from peaceful river valleys along the Ucero and Abión rivers to the dramatic rocky cliffs and caves of the nearby Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park. You'll also find paths with views of historic sites like Osma Castle and the medieval town surroundings.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by historical landmarks or points of interest?
A: Many routes incorporate the rich history of the area. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150718" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burgo de Osma – View of Osma Castle loop&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the historic castle. You can also find routes near the ancient Roman bridge over the Ucero River, or explore the surroundings of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1072360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Saint Bartholomew&lt;/a&gt; within the Cañón del Río Lobos.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Burgo De Osma?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064160854/way-of-the-cid-castillejo-de-robledo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Way of the Cid: Castillejo de Robledo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through varied rural landscapes. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150718" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burgo de Osma – View of Osma Castle loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find running routes that are suitable for families with children?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes aren't explicitly categorized, the region offers many 'short walks, with little unevenness and without difficulties' around Burgo de Osma, particularly along the riverbanks. These easy paths are generally suitable for families looking for a gentle jog or stroll.

Q: Are there any challenging or advanced running trails for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a challenge, there are several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064198016/gullurias-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gullurías Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 6.4-mile route with notable elevation changes, offering views towards the Cañón del Río Lobos. Another challenging option is the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Lobos – Hermitage of Saint Bartholomew loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Burgo De Osma?
A: The running routes in Burgo De Osma are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.3 stars. Over 300 runners have explored the varied terrain, often praising the scenic river valleys and the unique blend of natural beauty with historic surroundings.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064198016/gullurias-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gullurías Trail&lt;/a&gt; provides excellent views towards the Cañón del Río Lobos. Within the Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3634900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gullurías Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; itself offers panoramic vistas of the Soria and Burgos pine forests and the peaks of the Iberian System.

Q: Can I find running routes near water features like rivers or lakes?
A: Yes, the region is defined by the confluence of the Ucero and Abión rivers, so many routes follow their banks. You can also find routes near lakes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2677048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vildé Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7040471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pozo Perín&lt;/a&gt;, offering refreshing scenery for your run.

Q: Are there any specific routes that highlight the Gorge of the River Abión?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064181923/rio-abion-gorge-and-la-pedriza-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Abión Gorge and La Pedriza Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.1-mile path that specifically winds through the scenic Gorge of the River Abión, offering a beautiful and engaging running experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Burgo De Osma?
A: Burgo De Osma offers pleasant conditions for jogging for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer mornings and evenings are also ideal to avoid the midday heat, while winter can offer crisp, clear runs, especially on the well-maintained paths.
tips: The Church of Our Lady of the Assumption in Castillejo de Robledo is a 12th-century Romanesque temple, frequently attributed to the Templars although without definitive proof.

Castillejo de Robledo Castle is a medieval fortress located in the town of Castillejo de Robledo. This castle has a rich history, as it was built on an old Muslim fortification in the 12th century and later became the property of the Knights Templar.
The castle is notable for its double walled enclosure and its towers, including an irregularly shaped keep and a pentagonal tower that rises above a cliff. Although it is currently in ruins, it remains a site of great historical and architectural interest.
Along this route, you'll find numerous fields and landscapes reminiscent of medieval times and the exploits of El Cid Campeador.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the circular running route?
faqs.answer: The circular route starts and ends in Castillejo de Robledo. You should be able to find parking within the village, which serves as the primary access point for this trail.

faqs.question: Is this circular running route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through rural areas and agricultural lands, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to ensure their safety and respect local wildlife and livestock.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to run this route are typically spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is milder, making for more comfortable conditions. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially challenging terrain conditions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to run this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and run this circular route around Castillejo de Robledo, including the sections along the Camino del Cid.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The route features varied terrain, including rural roads and a small mountain path. You'll traverse landscapes with riverine vegetation, agricultural plains, vineyards, juniper forests, and limestone slopes of small ravines.

faqs.question: What historical landmarks and natural features can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in history and natural beauty. You'll encounter the impressive ruins of Castillejo de Robledo Castle, an ancient Templar fortress, and the beautiful Romanesque Church of Our Lady of the Assumption. The trail also winds through fields on the Camino del Cid, offering scenic views of agricultural plains and juniper forests.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginner runners?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate' for jogging, this 8 km circular route is best suited for individuals with a good fitness level. While not overly long, the varied terrain and small mountain path might be challenging for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route incorporates significant sections of the historic Camino del Cid. Specifically, it overlaps with the Camino del Cid (Senderista) and the GR 160 - Camino del Cid BTT for approximately 47% of its length, and also touches upon the Camino del Cid for about 41%.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or pubs in Castillejo de Robledo?
faqs.answer: Castillejo de Robledo is a small village, but it typically offers basic amenities. You may find a local bar or a small shop for refreshments before or after your run. It's advisable to check opening hours, especially outside of peak season.

faqs.question: What is the historical significance of this area to the Camino del Cid?
faqs.answer: Castillejo de Robledo is a pivotal location on the Camino del Cid, deeply connected to the epic poem "Cantar de Mio Cid." Many scholars believe this is the traditional site of the legendary "Afrenta de Corpes" (Affront of Corpes), a significant event in the story of El Cid Campeador.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular running route?
faqs.answer: This 8 km circular route is estimated to take approximately 55 minutes to complete at a jogging pace. However, your actual time may vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the historical sites and scenery.
